closed.title: Fix stale staging cookie on logout

mergedPRs.title: fix(crouton-auth): clear session cookie on the staging domain

epics.title: Standing rule: deploy to staging, never production by default
epics.whatItIs: Make staging the default deploy target everywhere, so an agent or a routine push can never ship to production by accident — production stays a deliberate, manual action.
epics.whereWeAre: The default staging script is in. The production gate and the CI guard are still open, and the whole thing is stuck waiting on a production API token with the right scope.
epics.recentActivity: Blocked on the production token scope — waiting on account access.

children.title: Add cf:staging as the default deploy script
Gate /deploy-production behind an explicit human request
Add a CI guard that refuses prod from a non-dispatch trigger

epics.title: Recursive task-decomposition pipeline (epic → sub-issues → workers)
epics.whatItIs: Let one fuzzy task be broken into a tree of GitHub issues automatically and worked by agents end-to-end — orchestrator splits the epic, decomposers recurse, workers build the leaves on isolated branches.
epics.whereWeAre: Almost done — the orchestrator, recursive decomposer, worktree-isolated worker, and the leaf-test stop conditions all merged. Only tuning the depth and fan-out defaults is left.
epics.recentActivity: Worker isolation via git worktrees merged yesterday; only the depth-cap tuning is left.

epics.title: Daily epic-progress digest: an at-a-glance email of what moved and what's left
epics.whatItIs: This very digest — a daily at-a-glance email of where every epic stands and what moved in the last 24h, so you don't have to piece it together from the issues board.
epics.whereWeAre: Just kicked off. The render-only skill (HTML + text) is the first sub-issue and is in progress now; email sending and scheduling come after.
epics.recentActivity: Epic opened today; the render-only skill is the first sub-issue, in progress now.
